what_it_does: Acceptance test script for the Suite lane over the corpus pillar. It builds a fresh Suite over a temporary FsStore, verifies that cognition selects surface the file-discovered projection registry, round-trips corpus records through the Suite face with lineage enforcement, lists/filters corpus records, computes near-minus-far relation inversions using seeded deterministic vectors, and confirms those methods are not in the RHM verb surface.
observations: Top-level script with a global PASS counter and small check/raises helpers.
Discovers nodes from the repo nodes directory and projections from the repo projections directory.
Creates and removes a temporary projection file under projections/ to prove drop-in lens discovery.
Seeds 2-D vectors directly into the store because the embedder service is expected to be down.
Includes an explicit 'C' teeth-check to prove the far-space subtraction filter is active.
Asserts that find_relations and corpus methods are absent from suite.RHM_VERBS.
Uses deterministic corpus_address and checks lineage gate raises CorpusError.
standouts: The 'THE BAR' drop-in projection test writes and removes a file in the source projections directory as a bridge-restart analog.
Deterministic vector geometry with a dedicated C item to prove the ¬far subtraction has teeth.
Floor assertion separating read/telemetry corpus and relation methods from RHM verbs.
conventions: Acceptance-test style with lightweight check/raises helpers rather than a framework runner.
Imports after sys.path manipulation marked with noqa: E402.
Docstring maps each assertion to a GROUP B/D law and labels the file as a FACE over a pillar.
Uses a temporary directory for the store and cleans it up in a finally block.
concerns: The test mutates the source projections/ directory at runtime; an interruption could leave acc_suite_tmp_lens.py behind.
Relies on a specific set of seeded projection ids and on the repo nodes directory existing.
Top-level global mutable state (PASS) and broad os.chdir/sys.path side effects.
Vector seeding uses hardcoded dimensions and model names tied to the test geometry.
notes: Executable acceptance contract that the Suite's projection selects, corpus persistence, and relation inversion stay thin, discoverable, fail-loud, and off the MCP verb surface.
questions: What prevents concurrent runs from colliding on the temporary projection file name?
How does the test behave if the projections directory already contains lenses not in the assumed seed set?
Where is RHM_VERBS defined and what does membership imply?
Are the seeded vectors cleaned up, or is cleanup only via rmtree of the temp store?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the file: it proves projection registry discovery, corpus round-trip/filtering, relation inversion, and floor separation.
apparent_themes: Registry-is-truth: selects must project discovered files, not hardcoded lists.
File-discovery: dropping a lens file makes it appear without code edits.
Thin reuse: Suite methods delegate to runtime/corpus and query_index.
Fail-loud: lineage gate and missing anchor raise exceptions.
Floor separation: read/telemetry methods are not RHM verbs.
intention_signals: To provide a deterministic, model-free acceptance proof that the Suite correctly surfaces discovered projections, persists and retrieves corpus records with lineage, filters corpus lists, computes near-minus-far vector inversions, and keeps those capabilities below the MCP verb surface.
novelty: The drop-in projection file as a bridge-restart analog and the explicit vector-C teeth-check to prove the far-space subtraction is actually enforced.
contribution: An executable acceptance contract that guards projection discovery against hardcoding, corpus persistence against missing lineage, relation inversion against losing the far filter, and the Suite read surface against accidental elevation to RHM verbs.
summary_for_embedding: suite_corpus_relations_acceptance.py is an acceptance test that validates a Suite discovers projection lenses from files, round-trips corpus records with lineage, filters corpus lists, computes near-minus-far vector inversions via seeded vectors, and keeps those methods off the RHM verb surface.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the Suite lane over the corpus pillar obeys registry-is-truth, thin reuse, fail-loud lineage, deterministic relation geometry, and floor separation without a live embedding model.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments PASS and prints ok for each passing check.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts the supplied condition, increments the global PASS counter on success, prints an 'ok' line, and fails with a labeled message if the condition is false. It is the primary verification primitive used throughout the acceptance script.

symbols.does: Exception-expectation helper verifying a callable raises a specified exception type.
symbols.description: raises(label, fn, exc=Exception) invokes fn inside a try block and checks that it raises exc; if so it records a pass via check, otherwise it records a failure. It is used to prove the lineage gate and the missing-anchor failure modes.

symbols.does: Factory that constructs a fresh Suite over a temporary FsStore with discovered nodes.
symbols.description: new_suite(root) creates an FsStore under the given root, discovers nodes from the repository nodes directory via NodeRegistry, and returns a new Suite instance. It is used to simulate a bridge-restart and ensure each test phase sees a freshly built Suite.

symbols.does: Deterministic vector loader that stores a 2-D vector for an item in a named space.
symbols.description: seed(item, space, vec) writes a vector directly into the store under the item's space address, bypassing the live embedder. It sets up the deterministic geometry used by the find_relations near-minus-far inversion test.
